title: Resolve any workspace's anchor cwd via the D10 join, degrading instead of panicking
action: Implement the anchor resolution locked as D10, as a pure function on Snapshot with no I/O. EXACT SIGNATURE: pub fn anchor_cwd_for_workspace(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Option<String>, returning the resolved folder, None on any miss. Do not return the pane itself; slice 4 adds a pane-id-returning variant if it needs one. THE JOIN: workspace_id -> that workspace's active_tab_id -> the layouts[] entry whose workspace_id AND tab_id BOTH match -> its focused_pane_id -> that pane in the snapshot's top-level panes[] -> its foreground_cwd if set, else its cwd. Matching a layouts[] entry on tab_id alone happens to work on today's data because herdr's public tab ids are globally unique (w3:t6), but D10 states both keys and both must be matched. Resolve focused_pane_id against the snapshot's top-level panes[], never against a layout's own inner panes[] -- that is a geometry array and carries no cwd. This reproduces herdr's focused_pane_cwd_in_workspace (upstreams/herdr/src/app/creation.rs:55-58) and its foreground-then-cwd precedence (upstreams/herdr/src/workspace/tab.rs:575-583). Read D10's rationale before writing it: the obvious-looking shortcut is wrong and was caught by review. The snapshot's top-level focused_workspace_id/focused_tab_id/focused_pane_id are GLOBAL -- they describe only the one active workspace -- as is the per-pane focused flag. Using them would leave every non-focused workspace, the normal case, with no anchor. They are useful only for preselecting a default destination, never for resolving an anchor. Two degrade paths are real and must return None rather than guessing: herdr drops a whole layouts[] entry when its focused pane has no public id (upstreams/herdr/src/app/api/panes.rs:1686), and active_tab_id falls back to an id synthesized from the tab index plus one (upstreams/herdr/src/app/creation.rs:453-456) which can disagree with the real public tab number, since those come from a monotonic counter that never reuses a closed tab's number (upstreams/herdr/src/workspace.rs:510-511, 978-979, read via public_tab_number at :1025). Neither path can yield the wrong pane, only no pane. Follow the existing fallback discipline at wire.rs:133-151 and the join-miss tests at wire.rs:268-303. USE THE REAL CAPTURE: .bee/spikes/new-shell-new-agent/live-snapshot.json is a real herdr envelope with 5 workspaces of which 4 are not globally focused, and whose focused workspace's anchor is a plain shell absent from agents[]. src/herdr/testdata/expected-anchors.json records the expected anchor for every one of those workspaces -- a live probe resolved all 5, every one via foreground_cwd. Assert against those known-good values rather than inventing expectations. Ignore .bee/spikes/new-shell-new-agent/findings.json: it predates the home-path neutralization and its cwd strings disagree with the fixture. TEST NAMING IS PART OF THE CONTRACT: every test you add for this cell must be named with the prefix anchor_ (for example anchor_resolves_for_non_focused_workspace, anchor_prefers_foreground_cwd, anchor_live_envelope_matches_probe). The cell's verify counts passing anchor_ tests and requires at least 8; it deliberately fails on an untouched tree, so a green verify is evidence the work exists rather than evidence the suite was already green. FIXTURE PATHS: cell new-shell-new-agent-1 commits src/herdr/testdata/live-snapshot.json and src/herdr/testdata/expected-anchors.json as tracked test data; use those via include_str!, never the .bee/spikes/ copies, which are gitignored and absent in CI. expected-anchors.json records, per workspace, the expected_cwd, anchor_pane_id, active_tab_id, resolved_via, is_globally_focused and anchor_is_plain_shell_absent_from_agents -- assert against those rather than inventing expectations. NOTE the live capture has no pane with a null cwd, so the null-cwd degrade truth needs a small hand-written fixture of its own; hand-writing is correct for degrade cases, the no-hand-written-fixture rule applies only to the main happy-path envelope.

truths: The anchor resolves for a workspace that is NOT the globally focused one, in a fixture containing at least two workspaces where the top-level focused_workspace_id and focused_pane_id point at the other workspace and would yield a different pane
The globally focused workspace also resolves
Against the tracked fixture src/herdr/testdata/live-snapshot.json, every one of its 5 workspaces resolves to the expected_cwd recorded for it in src/herdr/testdata/expected-anchors.json
A layouts[] entry whose tab_id matches but whose workspace_id does not is never used
foreground_cwd takes precedence over cwd when both are present
cwd is used when foreground_cwd is absent
An anchor pane that is a plain shell, absent from agents[], still resolves via panes[]
A layouts[] entry missing for the active tab yields None, not a panic and not a different pane
A snapshot with no layouts[] at all yields None
A pane with both cwd and foreground_cwd null yields None
artifacts: pub fn anchor_cwd_for_workspace(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Option<String> on Snapshot in src/herdr/wire.rs, pure and I/O-free
A test driving the tracked fixture src/herdr/testdata/live-snapshot.json via include_str! and asserting all 5 workspaces resolve to the expected_cwd values in src/herdr/testdata/expected-anchors.json
Tests covering every row of the slice 1 test matrix in docs/history/new-shell-new-agent/plan.md, including both degrade paths and the workspace_id-mismatch case
At least 8 tests named with the anchor_ prefix, all passing
key_links: docs/history/new-shell-new-agent/CONTEXT.md D10 (the join) and D5 (the foreground-then-cwd precedence)
src/herdr/wire.rs:268-303 -- the join-miss fallback pattern to follow
src/herdr/testdata/expected-anchors.json -- known-good anchors derived from the tracked fixture itself
prohibitions: Do not resolve the anchor from focused_workspace_id, focused_tab_id, focused_pane_id, or a per-pane focused flag
Do not match a layouts[] entry on tab_id alone -- match workspace_id AND tab_id
Do not resolve focused_pane_id against a layout's inner panes[] -- use the snapshot's top-level panes[]
Do not fall back to the first tab's root pane -- that is how herdr derives the LABEL, not the anchor, and D11 keeps them separate on purpose
Do not panic or unwrap on any join miss
Do not call herdr or perform any I/O in this function
Do not add a web endpoint or any UI -- those are slices 4 and 5
